title: Doctor hook_sources: explicitly name the dual-source state (plugin projection + repo fallback both present) with a both-present regression test
action: Per D5+D9: doctorHookSourcesCodex (vendored at .bee/bin/bee.mjs:4779-4791; edit the CANONICAL skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs) currently reports presence with active:unknown and never names the dual-source risk. Extend the row: when BOTH hooks/hooks.json (plugin projection, a conformance-asserted rendered target per test_conformance.mjs:427 — never deleted) AND .codex/hooks.json (repo fallback) are present, the row's message explicitly states that two hook sources exist on disk, that exactly-one-active is the law (hook-source-exclusivity B14), that the current premise is capability-matrix row B1 (plugin hooks not-observed on the probed codex version), and that the premise must be re-proved when the probed version changes. Keep verdict semantics conservative (ok/warn as today — do not invent a blocking row; doctor stays read-only). Also have the row distinguish hooks/claude-hooks.json (the plugin.json-declared manifest) from hooks/hooks.json in its configured listing so the two Claude-shaped renders are named, closing #54 item 8 in the surfaced text. Add a regression test for the both-present scenario asserting the dual-source sentence appears (find doctor's existing test surface via rg 'doctorHookSources|hook_sources' across scripts/ and skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/ and extend it; create a focused test file only if none exists). Regen obligation inside THIS cell: node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write.

truths: doctor stays read-only and fail-closed verdict semantics unchanged
active: unknown honesty preserved — presence never inferred as active
both-present state is named in the row message with its premise
prohibitions: never delete or rename hooks/hooks.json or hooks/claude-hooks.json (D9)

trace.outcome: Extended doctorHookSourcesCodex (skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs) per D5/D9: when BOTH hooks/hooks.json (plugin projection) and .codex/hooks.json (repo fallback) are present, the row's evidence text now names the dual-source risk explicitly (two hook sources exist, hook-source-exclusivity B14 is the exactly-one-active law, current premise is capability matrix row B1 which must be re-proved when the probed codex version changes). Also distinguishes hooks/claude-hooks.json (plugin.json-declared Claude manifest) from hooks/hooks.json in both the configured listing (new claude_hooks_manifest_checked_in field) and the surfaced text, closing #54 item 8. Verdict semantics (ok/warn from repoPresent alone) and active:unknown honesty are untouched; no blocking flag added. Added a both-present regression test in test_bee_cli.mjs covering single-source baseline (no dual-source sentence) and both-present (dual-source sentence + B14 + row B1 + re-proof language + claude-hooks.json distinction), confirmed red against the pre-change function via git stash. Regenerated vendored trees (render_plugin_skill_trees, onboard --apply, release_manifest --write).
